concepts.site_goal: Position Fernwood Conservatory & Rare Plant Nursery as a destination garden and serious horticultural authority, while driving conservatory visits, plant purchases, workshop sign-ups, and patron memberships.
audience: Serious home gardeners and plant collectors
Patrons and members supporting plant conservation
concepts.description: An editorial, herbarium-inspired site for Fernwood Conservatory, a 14-acre specialty botanical garden and rare-plant nursery in the Pacific Northwest. The visual system mimics a leather-bound Victorian botanical folio updated for the web: hand-pressed botanical illustration headers, ruled column lines, deckled-edge image plates, and italic latinate captions. Mossy green dominates with bone-white pages and soft coral accents for in-bloom callouts. Long-scrolling encyclopedia entries sit beside an interactive bloom calendar and a hand-drawn SVG garden map. The shop and membership flows feel curatorial rather than commercial.
concepts.motif: Pressed-herbarium folio: deckled paper textures, taxonomic rule lines, hand-inked botanical illustration headers, hairline dividers, and italic Latin binomial captions over mossy green and bone white

pages.layout_pattern: Full-bleed illustrated hero plate over conservatory photo, followed by 'In Bloom This Week' coral-tagged carousel, a three-column editorial teaser grid, a mossy banded visit-planning strip, and a footer with herbarium-style index
sections: Illustrated hero with site name and tagline
In Bloom This Week carousel with month chip
Footer with garden hours, address, and indexed sitemap

pages.layout_pattern: Left filter rail with taxonomic facets, right grid of botanical-plate cards each with Latin binomial caption and conservation badge, alphabetical jump strip across the top
sections: Header with search input and alphabetical jump strip
Filter rail (family, hardiness zone, light, conservation status)

pages.layout_pattern: Two-column editorial layout: large illustration plate left, ruled prose right with sticky tab nav for Overview, Cultivation, Propagation, and In the Garden; related cultivars appear as small plates at the foot
sections: Quick facts ruled table (family, native range, hardiness, light, soil)
Sticky tabs (Overview / Cultivation / Propagation / In the Garden)

pages.layout_pattern: Centered circular SVG bloom wheel with month spokes and species arcs, month slider beneath, and a list view below showing currently peaking species with garden zone coordinates
sections: Heading with current month in italic Latin
Link to garden map for each species

pages.layout_pattern: Full-width interactive SVG map with mossy-green hand-drawn zones, right-rail zone detail panel that updates on hover or tap, legend along the bottom
sections: Map header with current bloom highlights toggle

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial month-view calendar at top, scrollable list of workshop cards below with instructor portrait roundels, skill-level chips, and remaining-seat badges
Filtered grid of nursery products with botanical-plate thumbnails, pot-size and provenance captions, soft coral 'Ships This Season' tags, and a left rail for filters
sections: Shop header with seasonal shipping notice
Filter rail (cultivar group, pot size, light, hardiness)

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial hero with patron testimonial, four-column tier table with ornament dividers, FAQ accordion below, and a footer band with patron honor roll names
sections: What your membership funds (conservation impact strip)

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial magazine grid with one large featured plate-essay and two-column secondary articles below, each tagged with cultivar or season, sidebar of recent dispatches
sections: Tag filter chips (cultivar, season, garden zone)

message_intent: Convey deep horticultural expertise and editorial seriousness
Invite physical visits to the glasshouses and grounds
Drive rare-plant nursery purchases and waitlist sign-ups
content_model: Cultivar encyclopedia entry (binomial name, common names, family, native range, hardiness, light/water/soil, propagation notes, conservation status, illustration plate, related species)
Bloom calendar event (species, peak window, garden zone, sun exposure, accompanying companions)
Garden zone (name, acreage, signature collection, accessibility notes, map coordinates)
Workshop session (title, instructor, date, duration, skill level, capacity, materials, price, member discount)
Editorial feature article (title, author, hero illustration, pull quotes, related cultivars)
Nursery product (cultivar, pot size, age, provenance, stock count, shipping season, price)
Membership tier (name, annual price, benefits list, guest passes, reciprocal gardens)
interactions: Month slider on bloom calendar that recolors a circular wheel of species in peak
Interactive SVG garden map with hover-highlighted zones and pinned cultivar markers
Encyclopedia search with filters for hardiness zone, light, family, and conservation status
Workshop calendar with month/list toggle and seat-count availability badges
Add-to-cart with seasonal shipping window notice and waitlist for sold-out cultivars
Sticky in-page tabs on cultivar pages (Overview / Cultivation / Propagation / In the Garden)
asset_needs: Hand-inked botanical illustrations for at least eight signature cultivars
Deckled-edge paper texture for plate frames
SVG hand-drawn garden map with labeled zones
Photography of glasshouses, beds, and workshop benches with warm overcast light
Botanical line-art ornaments and section dividers
concepts.mobile_behavior: Single-column reflow with the garden map becoming a pan-and-zoom panel; encyclopedia tabs collapse to an accordion; bloom wheel becomes a horizontally scrollable month strip

concepts.domain: Specialty rare-plant nursery and demonstration garden
concepts.site_goal: Establish Moss & Meridian as a contemporary specialty nursery and small demonstration garden — sell rare plants by season, schedule propagation workshops, and bring visitors to the greenhouse open days.
audience: Urban plant collectors and houseplant enthusiasts
Workshop students learning propagation and stewardship
concepts.description: A more contemporary, almost zine-like take on the botanical site: Moss & Meridian is a small-batch rare-plant nursery with a quarter-acre demonstration garden and a working glasshouse. The design pairs editorial horticulture with a modern grid: oversized italic display serifs, wide-tracked sans labels, and full-bleed photography of cuttings on linen. Mossy green panels sit against bone-white margins, with soft coral used for stock indicators and seasonal tags. The bloom calendar reads as a seasonal almanac, and the encyclopedia is organized by 'meridians' (latitudinal native bands) rather than family. Visitors can reserve open-day tickets, browse a catalog with propagation provenance, and follow a serialized 'Grower's Notebook'.
concepts.motif: Contemporary almanac: oversized italic serif headlines, tracked-out sans labels, hairline grid lines, and large-format photographs of cuttings on linen, with mossy green panels and soft coral seasonal tags

pages.layout_pattern: Full-bleed photograph of a propagation bench, oversized italic serif headline overlaid, asymmetric two-column intro, a 'This Week in the Glasshouse' band, and three feature blocks for catalog, workshops, and notebook
sections: Hero with headline and shipping-season note
This Week in the Glasshouse band
Footer with hours, address, and zone shipping note

pages.layout_pattern: Sticky header with postcode shipping-check, left filter rail with meridian band, light, container size, and price, a generous grid of large catalog cards with lineage drawers, and a quiet pagination footer
Large photo left, editorial spec column right with mossy green meridian tag, propagation lineage diagram below the fold, care guide accordions, and a related cultivars strip
sections: Editorial spec column (light, water, container, age)
Add to order with shipping window note

pages.layout_pattern: Horizontal week scrubber across the top, then a three-panel layout: what's flowering, what's dormant, garden tasks; below, a 12-week look-ahead timeline; mossy green panel bands separate sections
Hero with horizontal globe slider showing latitude bands, meridian band cards stacked below each linking to a band detail page with native climate notes and signature cultivars
sections: Signature cultivar plate strip per band

pages.layout_pattern: Season tabs at top (Spring / Summer / Autumn / Winter), workshop cards in a single column with hero photo, instructor roundel, materials list, and take-home plant chip; reservation modal launches from each
Hero photo of the gates, two-column intro with hours and directions, a hand-drawn nursery map with bench numbers, open-day ticket picker with time-slot tiles, and accessibility detail block
Editorial feed with one oversized featured entry at the top and a two-column reverse-chronological grid below, tag chips for cultivars and seasons, sidebar with author bio
Long editorial scroll: founding story column, glasshouse tour stops with photographs and microclimate captions, growing philosophy pull quotes, team portraits, and a closing values band

message_intent: Communicate craft, provenance, and small-batch growing
Drive online catalog orders during the active shipping seasons
Fill workshop seats and open-day reservations
Build a long-form following through the Grower's Notebook
required_text: Small-batch rare plants, grown from cutting
content_model: Catalog plant (cultivar, meridian/native band, lineage, propagation method, age, light, watering, container size, stock state, price)
Almanac entry (week, what's flowering, what's dormant, what's available, garden tasks)
Meridian region (latitudinal band, climate notes, signature cultivars, conservation considerations)
Workshop (title, instructor, date, hands-on materials, take-home plant, capacity, price)
Open-day session (date, time slot, capacity, ticket price, accessibility notes)
Grower's Notebook entry (date, author, hero photograph, body essay, tagged cultivars)
Glasshouse tour stop (name, plants in residence, microclimate notes)
interactions: Meridian selector that filters the encyclopedia by native latitude band on a horizontal globe slider
Almanac week scrubber that updates 'this week in the garden' text and a small inline plot
Open-day ticket picker with time-slot tiles and remaining-seat counters
Catalog cards with hover lineage drawers showing the cultivar's parent stock
Workshop reservation modal with materials checklist and dietary accessibility fields
Sticky 'currently shipping to your zone' postcode check in the catalog header
asset_needs: Large-format photography of cuttings, propagation trays, and the glasshouse interior
Botanical line illustrations of signature cultivars and meridian band diagrams
Linen, kraft, and paper textures for editorial panels
Hand-drawn nursery map with bench numbers and microclimate labels
Instructor portraits with consistent natural-light styling
concepts.mobile_behavior: Single-column stack with meridian globe collapsing into a horizontal band-picker; almanac scrubber becomes a swipeable week strip; catalog hover drawers convert to tap-to-expand panels
